items.title: Cross-border child protection hub aims to keep kids connected to families

children.content: A new hub to support families caught between three child protection agencies has been established in Alice Springs in a 12-month trial.

image.altText: Three Indigenous women sit side by side on chairs in a room and lean into each other for a photo.

items.title: NT child protection bill opponents show 'moral bankruptcy', MLA says

children.content: Fierce debate has surrounded the Northern Territory government's child protection reforms — which will return to parliament for debate next week — with one MLA saying those opposing it have shown "a degree of moral bankruptcy".

image.altText: A man in a navy jacket and pink tie gestures with his hands as he talks

items.title: NT government slammed over no-show to federal racism inquiry

children.content: A national inquiry into racism against First Nations people, launched after an alleged terror attack in Perth, has heard allegations of systemic racism in the Northern Territory, as the territory government comes under fire for snubbing the probe.

items.title: Hundreds face homelessness as accommodation centre set to close

children.content: Hundreds of residents fear they could be left homeless after the Northern Territory government announced it will shut a Darwin short-stay accommodation centre in less than 90 days.

image.altText: A young father and his son look at the camera.

items.title: 'I want the money that is owed to me': Charity fails to pay $150k in super

children.content: Former staff of charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation to the tune of $150,000. Under-18 staff also say the super accounts PAWS promised to create for them never existed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a white woman, with blonde hair tied back, black singlet top, sitting among greenery.

items.title: Health department refuses to move staff out of mould-riddled building

children.content: A Darwin building with a community health clinic inside has had to close off areas due to a years-long mould infestation across two floors.

image.altText: A three storey white building with a sign saying 'Casuarina Plaza'

items.title: 'Haven't had a day off all year': PM defends broken festival promise

children.content: A year ago the Anthony Albanese vowed to attend the Garma Festival every year for as long as he remained prime minister. Now he says he needs a break.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese, wearing a hat and no tie, addresses a crowd.

articles.title: No guarantee outages won't happen again, Telstra CEO says

children.content: The national Telstra outage last week was triggered by maintenance work that caused the system's clocks to go back two decades, the telco says.

image.altText: The orange and white telstra sign is in focus in the background as people walk past in a blur.

articles.title: Former diplomat blames Telstra landline service for repeated Triple Zero failures

children.content: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

image.altText: A man with short grey hair sits in a living room with a box labelled "Telstra Files" sitting on his lap.

children.content: Telstra CEO Vicki Brady is appearing before a Senate hearing over the over the nationwide outage, and potential compensation. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Telstra has issued credit worth $100,000 in compensation so far

updates.title: Thousands of Telstra customers faced Triple Zero call risk

items.title: Parents go against court orders after children 'traumatised'

children.content: The Family Court granted John full custody of his kids, but he returned them to their mother, saying the judge got it wrong.

image.altText: Illustration of young boy running to his mother, who is being hugged by daughter.

items.title: Universities were told of risks from violent intruders at campus protests

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has heard the tertiary sector's peak standards body wrote to universities to warn them about aggressive outsiders infiltrating pro-Palestinian protests.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder length brown hair, dark rimmed glasses and a black jacket sits behind a microphone.

items.title: UK urges FIFA to act after Argentine players hold up Malvinas banner

items.title: Treasurer says AI key to raising productivity, lowering interest rates

items.title: Drivers wait months for faults to be repaired on EVs built by new brands

items.title: 'Carry on patriots': Trump officials defend pilot after fighter jet stunt

children.content: Officials in US President Donald Trump's administration, including Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, continue to support low-altitude military flyovers despite growing safety concerns after a jet was filmed buzzing a crowded beach.

articles.title: ICE agent involved in fatal shooting violent and unstable, family says

articles.title: Smoke from Canada wildfires crosses into US prompting warnings to stay indoors

items.title: Australia's number one song now has AI credits on Spotify

items.title: Uber reverses 'disability tax' after outcry

children.content: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".

items.title: Ex-Wagga MP Daryl Maguire found guilty of conspiracy over fraudulent visas

items.title: Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving while disqualified
